title: Examples of Discrete-Time Systems
summary: This page derives the discrete-time accumulator or digital integrator equation from a sampled continuous-time integral, leading to y[n] = T∑_{k=-∞}^n x[k] and its equivalent difference equation form y[n] - y[n-1] = Tx[n]. It then introduces and contrasts recursive and nonrecursive forms of difference equations, emphasizing their computational differences while noting they represent the same system behavior.
